description:
## Parent

bsm-7en — Render selected issue details in the issue explorer

## Background and intent

Beadsmith is a Tauri + React desktop client for Beadwork. Beadwork remains the durable source of truth; Beadsmith should read structured `bw` JSON and present it without creating a second issue tracker. The current shipped slice renders the three-pane Issue Explorer chrome and the Issue List from `bw list --all --json`, but the data model and RPC/API names still describe that data as `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries`.

The parent epic establishes a more precise model: an **Issue** is the canonical Beadwork data loaded from the Current Workspace, while **Issue List** and **Issue Detail** are view-specific presentations of that same Issue. `bw list --all --json` already contains the fields needed for Issue Detail, including description and comments, so this epic should not add a new `bw show` call or detail-specific RPC.

This bead is the enabling prefactor. It keeps the user-visible Issue List behavior intact while renaming and expanding the contract so later beads can build Issue Detail without fighting summary-oriented names or inventing a second model.

## What to build

Rename the existing Issue List contract from `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` to `Issue` / `list_issues` across the Rust adapter/RPC boundary, generated TypeScript bindings, frontend Effect service, compact list view model, tests, and relevant documentation touched by this slice.

Expand the normalized Issue contract returned by the existing `bw list --all --json` path so every Issue includes the fields needed by future detail rendering:

- `description`: always-present string; missing/null raw descriptions become `""`.
- `comments`: always-present array; missing/null/omitted raw comments become `[]`.
- comment `author`: always-present string; missing author becomes `""` and is hidden later by view logic.
- `closeReason`: always-present string; missing/null raw close reasons become `""` and is hidden later by view logic.

Keep the current loading behavior and failure model exactly recognizable: loading, success with Issues, empty with workspace path, and typed failure.

## Reasoning and constraints

- Beadwork is the source of truth. Do not introduce local durable Issue state or a parallel Issue Detail model.
- Follow ADR-0003: consume structured `bw` JSON only; do not parse TTY/Markdown CLI output.
- Follow ADR-0004: Rust IPC goes through TauRPC/Specta, and React calls that through the Effect service pattern. Do not add direct `@tauri-apps/api/core.invoke` calls from React.
- Do not add a separate `bw show` call for this epic. The full Issue record should come from the existing list call.
- Preserve the raw Beadwork date/string values. Formatting is deliberately out of scope for this epic.
- This bead should be as behavior-preserving as possible except for contract shape and names.

## Testing guidance

Preserve the current adapter/RPC/Effect seams through the rename. Extend tests at those seams to prove the new fields are normalized from `bw list --all --json` and exposed through the generated/frontend contract. Keep compact Issue List view-model tests because the list still exists as a scan-oriented presentation over Issue data.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Existing `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` naming is replaced with `Issue` / `list_issues` across code, generated bindings, tests, and relevant docs touched by this slice.
- [ ] `bw list --all --json` remains the only Beadwork read used for the Issue List and future Issue Detail data.
- [ ] The normalized Issue contract includes always-present `description`, `comments`, comment `author`, and `closeReason` fields with empty defaults when Beadwork omits or nulls them.
- [ ] The raw Beadwork `defer_until` field continues to surface to the frontend as `deferUntil`.
- [ ] Existing list-loading states still behave as before: loading, success with Issues, empty with workspace path, and typed failure.
- [ ] Adapter tests prove the `bw list --all --json` invocation and normalization for description, comments, author, and closeReason.
- [ ] RPC/binding tests prove the expanded Issue contract is exported to TypeScript.
- [ ] Effect service tests are renamed/refactored to Issue/listIssues terminology and still cover success, empty, and failure behavior.
- [ ] Compact Issue List view-model tests are preserved under Issue terminology.
- [ ] No Issue selection, Markdown rendering, comments UI, dependency UI, or E2E expansion is implemented in this bead.

## Out of scope

- No `bw show` integration.
- No Issue Detail rendering.
- No Markdown renderer.
- No comment presentation.
- No dependency/metadata presentation beyond carrying existing fields in the Issue contract.
- No route, URL, history, refresh, or workspace-switch behavior.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately

comments.text:
# Implementation Plan

## Goal
Rename the issue-list contract from `IssueSummary`/`list_issue_summaries` to `Issue`/`list_issues` and thread `description`, `comments.author`, and `closeReason` through the existing `bw list --all --json` path without changing Issue List behavior.

## Tasks
1. **Preflight and scope check**: Prime the Beadwork workspace before editing.
   - File: none
   - Changes: Run `bw prime` from the repository root and confirm the worktree/context is safe to edit. Do not change `BW_PROGRAM`/`BW_ARGS`, do not add a `bw show` call, and do not render the new detail fields in the list UI.
   - Acceptance: Implementation starts from current Beadwork context and keeps the slice limited to contract rename + field propagation.

2. **Make raw Beadwork parsing tolerant for newly exposed detail fields**: Update raw schema docs and `description` null/missing handling.
   - File: `src-tauri/src/issues/raw.rs`
   - Changes: Update doc links from `IssueSummary` to `Issue`. Change `RawIssue.description` from `String` to `Option<String>` so missing/null values can normalize to `""` at the adapter boundary. Keep `RawIssue.comments: Option<Vec<RawComment>>`, `RawComment.author: Option<String>`, and `RawIssue.close_reason: Option<String>` as the raw nullable/omittable representation.
   - Acceptance: JSON with omitted or null `description`, `comments`, `comment.author`, and `close_reason` can deserialize and be normalized later.

3. **Rename and expand the Rust adapter contract**: Replace `IssueSummary` with `Issue` and add normalized detail fields.
   - File: `src-tauri/src/issues/adapter.rs`
   - Changes: Rename `IssueSummary` to `Issue`; add `IssueComment { text: String, author: String, timestamp: String }`; add `description: String`, `comments: Vec<IssueComment>`, and `close_reason: String` to `Issue`. Update `list_all_issues`, `interpret_output`, `parse_issues`, and `impl From<RawIssue> for Issue`. Map `raw.description.unwrap_or_default()`, `raw.comments.unwrap_or_default().into_iter().map(...)`, `raw_comment.author.unwrap_or_default()`, and `raw.close_reason.unwrap_or_default()`. Preserve existing normalization for labels, blocks, blocked_by, and optional dates/parent.
   - File: `src-tauri/src/issues/mod.rs`
   - Changes: Update module docs and re-export `Issue` instead of `IssueSummary`.
   - Acceptance: Adapter tests assert the representative fixture includes `description`, one `IssueComment` with author, and `close_reason`; new normalization coverage proves missing/null `description`, missing/null `comments`, null/missing `comment.author`, and null/missing `close_reason` produce always-present empty strings/lists.

4. **Rename and expand the TauRPC/Specta boundary**: Make the frontend-facing API `list_issues` returning `ListIssuesResponse` with `Issue` objects.
   - File: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
   - Changes: Rename trait method and resolver `list_issue_summaries` -> `list_issues`; rename `ListIssueSummariesResponse` -> `ListIssuesResponse`; rename RPC `IssueSummary` -> `Issue`; add RPC `IssueComment`; rename helper `list_issue_summaries_from_adapter` -> `list_issues_from_adapter`. Update `impl From<issues::Issue> for Issue` and add/comment-map `impl From<issues::IssueComment> for IssueComment` or inline mapping. Keep `IssueListError`/`IssueListErrorKind` names unchanged.
   - Acceptance: RPC tests call `list_issues_from_adapter`, assert the new fields are present/defaulted (`description`, `comments`, `comments[0].author`, `close_reason` -> TS `closeReason`), and `generates_typescript_bindings` asserts `export type Issue`, `export type IssueComment`, `export type ListIssuesResponse`, `description: string`, `comments: IssueComment[]`, `closeReason: string`, and `list_issues`; it should no longer assert old summary names.

5. **Regenerate generated TypeScript bindings**: Let Taurpc/Specta rewrite the generated file after Rust changes.
   - File: `src/rpc/bindings.ts`
   - Changes: Do not hand-edit. Run a Rust build/test that executes `router::<tauri::Wry>().into_handler()` so `export_to = "../src/rpc/bindings.ts"` regenerates the file. Commit the generated diff.
   - Acceptance: Generated bindings export `Issue`, `IssueComment`, `ListIssuesResponse`; router key and `ARGS_MAP` use `list_issues`; no `IssueSummary`, `ListIssueSummariesResponse`, or `list_issue_summaries` remains in this file.

6. **Rename the frontend Effect loading service**: Move summary-named service files and symbols to issue-named equivalents.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-summary-loader.ts` -> `src/issues/issue-loader.ts`
   - Changes: Rename exported types/functions/constants: `IssueSummaryLoadErrorKind` -> `IssueLoadErrorKind`, `IssueSummaryLoadError` -> `IssueLoadError`, `IssueSummaryLoadState` -> `IssueLoadState`, `IssueSummaryTransportClient` -> `IssueTransportClient`, `IssueSummaryTransport` -> `IssueTransport`, `ISSUE_SUMMARY_LOADING_STATE` -> `ISSUE_LOADING_STATE`, `loadIssueSummaryState` -> `loadIssueState`, `createTauRpcIssueSummaryTransport` -> `createTauRpcIssueTransport`, and `loadIssueSummaryStateFromTauRpc` -> `loadIssueStateFromTauRpc`. Update the transport method from `listIssueSummaries` to `listIssues`, import `Issue`/`ListIssuesResponse`, call `rpc.list_issues()`, and update fallback copy from “issue summaries” to “issues”.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-summary-loader.test.ts` -> `src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts`
   - Changes: Rename test imports/classes/describes/helpers to issue names; update fixture type to `Issue` and include required `description`, `comments`, and `closeReason` fields.
   - Acceptance: Loading/success/empty/failure state shapes remain identical except renamed TypeScript symbols and transport method; tests pass under the new file and symbol names.

7. **Rename the compact list view model**: Move summary-named view-model files and symbols to issue-named equivalents without logic changes.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-summary-view.ts` -> `src/issues/issue-view.ts`
   - Changes: Import `Issue`, rename `IssueSummaryViewModel` -> `IssueViewModel`, rename `toIssueSummaryViewModel` -> `toIssueViewModel`, and update helper parameter types. Keep `IssueTone`, `badgeToneFor`, `issueToneFor`, `dependencyLabelFor`, and all row-mapping behavior unchanged.
   - File: `src/issues/issue-summary-view.test.ts` -> `src/issues/issue-view.test.ts`
   - Changes: Rename imports/describes/test copy to issue names and add required `description`, `comments`, and `closeReason` fixture fields.
   - Acceptance: View-model tests prove dense row metadata and fallback-label behavior are unchanged.

8. **Update React app references**: Point the app at the renamed service, view model, and generated contract.
   - File: `src/App.tsx`
   - Changes: Import `ISSUE_LOADING_STATE`, `loadIssueStateFromTauRpc`, `IssueLoadState`, `toIssueViewModel`, and generated `Issue`. Update `workspaceTextFor`, `IssueRow`, `IssueListContent`, `useState`, and `useEffect` type/function references. Optionally update the loading subtext from “Reading Beadwork issue summaries…” to “Reading Beadwork issues…” to match the new terminology; do not otherwise alter rendering or introduce Issue Detail UI.
   - Acceptance: The Issue List renders the same rows and states, using only renamed imports/types.

9. **Update e2e direct IPC reference**: Rename the WebDriver sanity-check channel and local type alias.
   - File: `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts`
   - Changes: Replace doc comment `IssueSummary` with `Issue`, rename local `ListIssueSummariesResponse` to `ListIssuesResponse`, and change `core.invoke("TauRPC__list_issue_summaries")` to `core.invoke("TauRPC__list_issues")`.
   - Acceptance: The e2e success spec still verifies the real native issue-list path and rendered DOM through the renamed TauRPC command.

10. **Update slice documentation literal**: Keep docs aligned with the new IPC channel.
    - File: `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`
    - Changes: Replace `TauRPC__list_issue_summaries` with `TauRPC__list_issues` in the Issue List e2e explanation.
    - Acceptance: Docs no longer point agents at the old TauRPC channel.

11. **Remove/verify old names across in-scope files**: Search for stale summary symbols after all renames.
    - File: repository search over `src/`, `src-tauri/`, `e2e/`, and `docs/agents/`
    - Changes: Fix any remaining `IssueSummary`, `ListIssueSummaries`, `list_issue_summaries`, `TauRPC__list_issue_summaries`, and `issue-summary` references in in-scope application files. Ignore vendored Beadwork references under `docs/research/infra/beadwork/`.
    - Acceptance: A targeted grep/rg returns no stale summary contract references in the application slice.

## Files to Modify
- `src-tauri/src/issues/raw.rs` - raw schema docs and nullable `description` representation.
- `src-tauri/src/issues/adapter.rs` - adapter rename to `Issue`, new `IssueComment`, detail-field normalization, tests.
- `src-tauri/src/issues/mod.rs` - re-export/docs rename from `IssueSummary` to `Issue`.
- `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs` - TauRPC method/response/type rename, `IssueComment`, mapping, binding-generation tests.
- `src/rpc/bindings.ts` - generated TauRPC/Specta output after Rust build/test.
- `src/App.tsx` - renamed imports/types/functions for issue loading and view mapping.
- `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts` - direct IPC channel/type/comment rename.
- `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md` - direct IPC channel documentation rename.

## New Files
- `src/issues/issue-loader.ts` - renamed Effect issue loading service replacing `src/issues/issue-summary-loader.ts`.
- `src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts` - renamed loader tests replacing `src/issues/issue-summary-loader.test.ts`.
- `src/issues/issue-view.ts` - renamed compact list view-model replacing `src/issues/issue-summary-view.ts`.
- `src/issues/issue-view.test.ts` - renamed view-model tests replacing `src/issues/issue-summary-view.test.ts`.

## Files to Remove/Rename Away
- `src/issues/issue-summary-loader.ts` - remove after moving contents to `src/issues/issue-loader.ts`.
- `src/issues/issue-summary-loader.test.ts` - remove after moving contents to `src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts`.
- `src/issues/issue-summary-view.ts` - remove after moving contents to `src/issues/issue-view.ts`.
- `src/issues/issue-summary-view.test.ts` - remove after moving contents to `src/issues/issue-view.test.ts`.

## Dependencies
- Task 1 precedes all editing.
- Task 2 must happen before Task 3 normalization tests can pass.
- Task 3 must happen before Task 4 can map `issues::Issue` into RPC `Issue`.
- Task 4 must happen before Task 5 can regenerate correct TypeScript bindings.
- Tasks 6-8 depend on Task 5 so frontend imports compile against `Issue`, `ListIssuesResponse`, and `rpc.list_issues()`.
- Task 9 depends on Task 4 because the IPC channel name comes from the renamed TauRPC method.
- Task 11 should run after Tasks 2-10.

## Validation Checklist
- Run `bw prime` before implementation.
- Run `cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml` after Rust edits.
- Run `(cd src-tauri && cargo test)`; verify adapter/RPC tests pass and `src/rpc/bindings.ts` is regenerated.
- Run `pnpm test`; verify renamed Vitest suites pass.
- Run `pnpm dlx ultracite check`; if it reports fixable issues, run `pnpm dlx ultracite fix` and re-check.
- Run targeted stale-name search, excluding vendored Beadwork: `rg "IssueSummary|ListIssueSummaries|list_issue_summaries|TauRPC__list_issue_summaries|issue-summary" src src-tauri e2e docs/agents` and expect no matches.
- Build the desktop e2e binary with `pnpm e2e:build`.
- Run `pnpm e2e:issue-list` if `bw` and the WebDriver prerequisites are available; otherwise record why it was skipped.
- Check `git status --short` to confirm intended renames, generated binding update, and no staged files unless the implementation flow intentionally stages at the end.

## Risks
- Taurpc regeneration can leave `src/rpc/bindings.ts` stale until a Rust build/test runs; frontend tests should run only after regeneration.
- `RawIssue.description` currently being a non-optional `String` would fail null/missing normalization tests; it must become nullable or use equivalent custom deserialization.
- Generated bindings should not be hand-edited because Taurpc will overwrite them.
- File renames can leave stale import paths in `App.tsx` or tests; the targeted stale-name search is required.
- `IssueComment` must be a TauRPC/Specta-compatible IPC type at the RPC layer, not just an adapter-only struct.
- E2E validation depends on `bw` being available on `PATH` and the Tauri/WebDriver build environment being healthy.
- Vendored Beadwork sources under `docs/research/infra/beadwork/` contain unrelated `IssueSummary` symbols; do not treat those as stale Beadsmith contract references.

    },

title: Rename Issue List contract to Issue
